name: Network Provider Order
categoryDescription: Registry keys that cause code to execute at boot, logon, or via Windows subsystem hooks.
description: Order in which Windows network providers (SMB, WebDAV, etc.) are queried for authentication — controls which provider handles a given UNC path.
forensic_value: Inserting a malicious network provider at the top of the order causes it to intercept all network authentication attempts — credential theft at the network layer.
